# RAW test fixtures

`raw-manifest.json` is the fixture source of truth. It separates generated
LinearRaw inputs from real camera RAW files so synthetic coverage cannot be
mistaken for camera compatibility evidence.

`leica-m8.dng` is an unchanged Leica M8 CFA DNG downloaded from raw.pixls.us.
The contributor released the file under CC0 1.0 through the raw.pixls.us upload
agreement. Tests pin the original URL and SHA-256.

`linear.dng` is generated by `make_linear_dng.py`. The lossy LinearRaw DNG and
Sony ARW belong to the pinned `vendor/LibRaw-Wasm` submodule.

`webgpu-camera-matrix.json` pins one CC0 Bayer RAW from Nikon, Panasonic, and
Fujifilm. `npm run test:webgpu-camera-matrix` downloads them from raw.pixls.us,
verifies their byte lengths and SHA-256 digests, and then compares every RGB16
TIFF channel from the production LibRaw and experimental WebGPU AAHD exports.
The downloaded files live in the ignored `webgpu-camera-matrix/` directory and
are never committed. Fujifilm X-A5 is deliberately used instead of the more
common X-Trans models because the experimental AAHD backend supports Bayer CFA
inputs only.
